data.Total Estimated Jobs Available: Up to 10,000 per query (API's own SearchResultCountAll ceiling); true federal total may exceed this across partitioned queries
data.Additional Supported Regions: N/A - US federal positions only, by design; no international federal listings exist to add
data.API Limits: Hard result window: exactly 10,000 results per query (confirmed live), regardless of ResultsPerPage. No published numeric rate limit - documented as "rate limited per User-Agent string." Latency scales with page size (~13s at 5,000/page vs ~2s at 500/page), which is why 500 was chosen over the tested maximum of 5,000.
data.Incremental Sync: Yes - DatePosted (days-based) filter is supported by the API but currently unused.
data.Date-based Sync: Yes: DatePosted parameter exists and is documented, but not used in the current implementation.
data.Unique Job Identifier: Not captured in the normalized schema, but the raw MatchedObjectDescriptor almost certainly carries a MatchedObjectId/PositionID per the documented API - only PositionURI (the canonical announcement URL) is extracted today.

data.Additional Supported Regions: N/A - single national index (Germany/Austria only); no other country is exposed by this API
data.API Limits: Hard result window: page*size cannot exceed 10,000, confirmed live. No published rate limit; occasional transient timeouts handled with exponential backoff (2s, 4s...). Auth is a well-known public, non-secret X-API-Key.
data.Incremental Sync: Yes - veroeffentlichtseit (days-since-published) filter is supported by the API but currently unused.
data.Date-based Sync: Yes: veroeffentlichtseit parameter exists and is documented, but not used in the current implementation.
data.Unique Job Identifier: refnr (reference number) - used directly in code to construct the canonical detail-page URL when no external URL is provided. A genuine, stable, provider-issued identifier.

data.Limitations / Rate Limits: Aggregation layer inside Google Search that crawls JobPosting schema.org markup across the open web. No public API to post jobs and no official public API to read/pull the aggregated results — the former Google Cloud Talent/Jobs API is discontinued; programmatic access only via scraping (against ToS).
data.Docs Available: Yes (JobPosting structured-data guidelines on Google Search Central)
data.Notes: Included for completeness: aggregates via schema.org JobPosting markup, not a feed you submit. No public read API (the old Cloud Talent/Jobs API was retired); no posting API. Verified on https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/job-posting and https://scrapfly.io/blog/posts/guide-to-google-jobs-api-and-alternatives | Access detail: Free to appear (organic, via schema.org markup); no paid or programmatic ingest API
data.Status: No Public Read API (schema.org crawl-based aggregator)
data.Additional Supported Regions: Most countries where Google Search operates
